Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation
Off Calendar Apr-21-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
John Glugloski, Esq. (415.983.0900; jglugoski@righettilaw.com) of Righetti Glugoski, P.C. for Plaintiff.
Dipanwita Amar, Esq. (415.471.3100; Dipanwita.Amar@arnoldporter.com) of Arnold & Porter for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS STERLING BANK & TRUST, FSB and STERLING BANCORP. INC.'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation.

Defendants' petition to compel arbitration is denied without prejudice, but its motion to stay the litigation is granted. This is a Private Attorneys General Act case. California law holds PAGA actions like this one not arbitrable. However, the United States Supreme Court may well resolve the issue within the next few weeks in Viking Rivers Cruises.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
